concerns.text: Task T008 specifies performing the ablation study on 'a subset' of data. The Plan (Summary) and FR-001 require a 'full ablation study' on the training set to generate ground-truth labels. No task exists to perform the ablation on the full training set, leaving the ground-truth generation for the classifier incomplete.

Task T009 states the model trains on 'validated static logs (proxy)' and explicitly notes 'NOT ablation data directly'. This contradicts FR-001, Constitution Principle VI, and the Plan, which mandate training on 'ablation-derived' ground-truth utility. No task exists to train the classifier on the ablation-derived labels generated by T008.

Task T025 implements McNemar's test. The Plan (Technical Context) and FR-005 note that if trajectories diverge (non-deterministic), McNemar's is invalid and a 'Permutation Test or Z-test' is required. No task exists to implement the divergence detection logic or the fallback statistical test, leaving the analysis invalid for diverging trajectories.

Task T016 defines the error condition for exceeding the token budget as a 'fallback to full layers'. The Spec (Edge Cases) and FR-003 require the system to 'truncate or prune' layers to stay within the 4096 token budget. No task exists to implement the truncation/pruning logic required to satisfy the hard budget constraint.

Task T005 mentions handling NaN/Inf edge cases but does not specify the required fallback behavior. The Spec (Edge Cases) mandates that on NaN/Inf, the system MUST 'default to retrieving the full all-layers set' and 'log a warning event'. No task explicitly covers implementing this specific fallback and logging requirement.

Task T022a only requires 'logging' whether the 30% token reduction threshold (SC-002) is met. SC-002 requires verifying this reduction. A task is missing to formally assert this threshold (e.g., fail the build or produce a verification artifact) to ensure the success criterion is testable and enforceable.
